topProducers.notes: Escondida (BHP/Rio Tinto) world's largest copper mine; water stress in Atacama a growing constraint
Las Bambas, Cerro Verde, Antamina; frequent community-related operational disruptions
Fastest-growing major producer; Kamoa-Kakula is world's highest-grade large copper deposit
Significant domestic consumption; China also largest processor of imported copper concentrates
Morenci and Bingham Canyon key operations; permitting timelines constrain new development

processingConcentration: China refines approximately 45% of global copper; Chilean and Zambian smelters process significant volumes. Concentrate-to-refined copper processing concentrated in Asia.
geopoliticalNotes: Chilean water access disputes in Atacama threatening expansion of key mines
Peruvian community protests causing recurring production disruptions at Las Bambas and others
DRC political instability and infrastructure constraints on Kamoa-Kakula ramp-up
Average copper mine grade declining from 1.7% in 1900 to 0.5% today — processing costs rising
10-15 year permitting and development timelines constrain supply response to high prices

majorCompanies.name: BHP Group
majorCompanies.notes: Escondida operator; Oak Dam expansion planned

majorCompanies.name: Codelco
majorCompanies.notes: State-owned Chilean national copper company; largest individual producer

majorCompanies.name: Ivanhoe Mines
majorCompanies.notes: Kamoa-Kakula operator; fastest-growing tier-1 copper producer

tradeFlowNotes: Copper concentrate flows from South American and African mines to Asian smelters (primarily China, Japan, South Korea). Refined copper cathode and rod then distributed to manufacturers globally.
